task: Port 14-format golf scoring engine from 'main' branch into the 'claude/master-spec-consolidation-Y6XjM' production branch

production_codebase.data_layer: Prisma server actions in src/actions/ (NOT API routes for most reads)
schema_notes: Round.status uses RoundStatus enum (DRAFT | LIVE | FINISHED) — NOT isLocked boolean
Team uses roundPlayers: RoundPlayer[] relation — NOT players: TeamPlayer[]
HoleScore uses holeNumber: Int directly — NOT a holeId FK
Format model has only: id, name, description (no defaultTeamSize)
Round model has NO formatConfig field

source_codebase.format_catalog: lib/format-definitions.ts — 14 format definitions with full metadata
source_codebase.scoring_engine: lib/scoring-engine.ts — pure scoring functions, no DB access

ordered_tasks.title: Add schema fields and PlayerScore model
ordered_tasks.instructions: Make the following ADDITIVE changes only. Do not remove any existing fields or models.

changes.add_field: formatConfig  Json?  // format-specific config (shambleCountMode, segment formats, etc.)

changes.model_definition:
model PlayerScore {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  roundId     String
  teamId      String
  playerId    String
  holeNumber  Int
  grossScore  Int?
  extraData   Json?    // driveSelected, isMoneyBall, moneyBallLost, wolfRole, etc.
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt

  round       Round    @relation(fields: [roundId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  team        Team     @relation(fields: [teamId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  player      Player   @relation(fields: [playerId], references: [id])

  @@unique([roundId, teamId, playerId, holeNumber])
  @@index([roundId, holeNumber])
  @@index([teamId])
}
changes.note: Uses holeNumber Int (consistent with HoleScore in this codebase) NOT holeId FK

ordered_tasks.title: Update seed to include all 14 formats
instructions: Import FORMAT_DEFINITIONS from '../src/lib/format-definitions'.
After the existing course/hole seeding, add a loop that upserts each format:
for (const def of FORMAT_DEFINITIONS) {
await prisma.format.upsert({
where: { name: def.name },
update: { description: def.gameDescription, defaultTeamSize: def.defaultTeamSize },
create: { name: def.name, description: def.gameDescription, defaultTeamSize: def.defaultTeamSize },
The Format.id is auto-generated (cuid) in this schema, NOT seeded to a fixed value.
To resolve format IDs at runtime, always look up by name and cross-reference FORMAT_MAP by matching name.
Alternatively: seed formats with a fixed id matching FORMAT_DEFINITIONS[n].id by using createMany with skipDuplicates.

ordered_tasks.title: Enrich formats API response with code-definition metadata
ordered_tasks.note: FORMAT_MAP keys are format IDs (cuid from DB). Fallback to name matching handles the mismatch between seeded cuid IDs and definition string IDs.

ordered_tasks.title: Add player scores server action
ordered_tasks.instructions: Create a server action file with the following exports:

exports_to_implement.name: getPlayerScores
exports_to_implement.signature: async (roundId: string, options?: { holeNumber?: number; teamId?: string }): Promise<PlayerScore[]>
exports_to_implement.behavior: Return PlayerScore records from DB filtered by roundId. Optionally filter by holeNumber and/or teamId. Include player relation for display.

exports_to_implement.name: upsertPlayerScore
exports_to_implement.signature: async (entry: { roundId: string; teamId: string; playerId: string; holeNumber: number; grossScore: number | null; extraData?: Record<string, unknown> }): Promise<PlayerScore>
exports_to_implement.behavior: Prisma upsert on @@unique[roundId, teamId, playerId, holeNumber]. Revalidate the scoring path.

exports_to_implement.name: upsertPlayerScores
exports_to_implement.signature: async (entries: Array<{ roundId: string; teamId: string; playerId: string; holeNumber: number; grossScore: number | null; extraData?: Record<string, unknown> }>): Promise<{ updated: number }>
exports_to_implement.behavior: Run all upserts in a prisma.$transaction for atomicity.

ordered_tasks.title: Update round creation UI with format description and config options
instructions: Fetch formats from /api/formats (already done) — the enriched response now includes gameDescription, configOptions, requiresIndividualScores, formatCategory, supportedTeamSizes.
Extend the Format interface to include: gameDescription, formatCategory, supportedTeamSizes, configOptions, requiresIndividualScores, requiresDesignatedPlayer.
Add formatConfig state: Record<string, unknown> initialized to {}.
When the format dropdown changes:
- Call handleFormatChange(formatId)
- Build default config from selected format's configOptions defaultValues

Show the selected format's gameDescription in a blue info box below the format dropdown.
Render configOptions dynamically:
- type 'boolean' → checkbox
- type 'number' → number input
- type 'select' → select dropdown
For Irish Golf / 6-6-6 (match by name 'Irish Golf / 6-6-6'), render three extra dropdowns:
- Holes 1-6 Format, Holes 7-12 Format, Holes 13-18 Format

- All three must be set before the form can submit
- Store as formatConfig.segment1FormatId, segment2FormatId, segment3FormatId
Pass formatConfig to the createRound server action call.

ordered_tasks.title: Update scoring page with format-aware individual score entry
instructions: Import FORMAT_MAP from @/lib/format-definitions.
Import getPlayerScores and upsertPlayerScores from @/actions/player-scores.
Import getRotatingDesignatedPlayerId from @/lib/scoring-engine.
After loading the round, look up the format definition: const formatDef = FORMAT_MAP.get(round.format.id) ?? [...FORMAT_MAP.values()].find(d => d.name === round.format.name)
If formatDef?.requiresIndividualScores is true, load player scores via getPlayerScores(roundId).
Add playerScores state: Map<string, number|null> where key is `${playerId}-${holeNumber}`.
For formats with requiresIndividualScores:
- For the current hole, render a per-player score entry UI for each team
- Each player gets a row with: player name, - button, score display (or '-'), + button, X button
- Score display shows gross score vs par (e.g., '+1', 'E', '-1', 'Birdie')
- On score change, call upsertPlayerScores with the updated gross score
- Keep the existing X / number entry UI visible alongside (for the team-level skins score if format is skins, or hide it for non-skins formats)
If formatDef?.requiresDesignatedPlayer is true:
- Show a banner: 'Designated Player: [playerName]' using getRotatingDesignatedPlayerId
- For money_ball format: show a 'Money Ball Lost' toggle button. When toggled, save moneyBallLost: true in the designated player's extraData via upsertPlayerScores
If formatDef?.requiresDriveTracking is true:
- For the designated drive player, show 'Drive used by [player]'
- Allow selecting which player's drive was used by showing a drive button per player

ordered_tasks.title: Update summary page with format-aware leaderboard
instructions: Import FORMAT_MAP from @/lib/format-definitions.

Import compute2BestBalls, compute3BestBalls, computeLoneRanger, computeMoneyBall, computeChaChaCha, computeShamble, computeChicagoTeamPoints, computeTrainGame, getRotatingDesignatedPlayerId from @/lib/scoring-engine.
Import getPlayerScores from @/actions/player-scores.
Look up the format definition: const formatDef = FORMAT_MAP.get(round.format.id) ?? [...FORMAT_MAP.values()].find(d => d.name === round.format.name).
If formatDef?.requiresIndividualScores is true, load player scores via getPlayerScores(roundId).
For non-skins formats, compute team totals by iterating holes and calling the appropriate scoring engine function per team per hole:
- Build PlayerInput[] for each team on each hole from the player scores data
- Call computeFormatScore(effectiveFormatId, players, holeNumber, par, {}, round.formatConfig) which dispatches to the right function
- For Irish Golf 6-6-6: use getIrishGolfSegmentFormatId(holeNumber, round.formatConfig) to get the sub-format per hole
Display a team leaderboard:
- Stroke formats: sort ascending by total gross score
- Points formats (Chicago): sort descending by total points
- Train Game: show 3-digit train numbers per hole, sum as total
Display Money Ball dual score table if format is money_ball:
- Columns: Team | Competition Total | Money Ball Total | MB Losses | Penalty Strokes
The existing skins summary (player payouts, hole-by-hole skin values) should remain unchanged for skins format.
For non-skins, replace the skin payout table with the team leaderboard described above.

ordered_tasks.title: Copy scoring engine tests
instructions: Copy the test file from the main branch.
Update the import path from '@/lib/scoring-engine' — the tsconfig path alias should resolve correctly already.
Run: npm test — all tests should pass.

important_architectural_notes.note: In this codebase Format.id is a cuid generated at seed time — NOT the string IDs like 'two_best_balls_of_four' from FORMAT_DEFINITIONS. To match a DB format to a FormatDefinition, look up by name: FORMAT_MAP.values().find(d => d.name === dbFormat.name). Alternatively, seed formats with explicit IDs matching the FORMAT_DEFINITIONS id strings by using prisma.format.upsert({ where: { id: def.id }, create: { id: def.id, ... } }). The second approach is strongly preferred as it makes FORMAT_MAP.get(format.id) work directly everywhere.
The production HoleScore model uses holeNumber: Int directly (not a FK to a Hole model). The new PlayerScore model should follow the same convention: use holeNumber: Int, not holeId: String.

Teams in this codebase use roundPlayers: RoundPlayer[] not players: TeamPlayer[]. When building PlayerInput[] for the scoring engine, iterate team.roundPlayers and map { playerId: rp.playerId, playerName: rp.player.fullName, ... }.

important_architectural_notes.topic: Server actions preferred over API routes
important_architectural_notes.note: This codebase primarily uses Next.js Server Actions in src/actions/ for data mutations. The scoring page calls upsertHoleScore() as a server action, not a fetch. New player score mutations should follow the same pattern via src/actions/player-scores.ts.
The existing skins scoring engine in src/lib/scoring-engine.ts handles calculateRoundResults, calculatePlayerPayouts, etc. Do not modify or remove these. Only append the new format functions.
Use round.status === 'FINISHED' to check if a round is complete (not round.isLocked). The summary page is only shown for FINISHED rounds.

verification_checklist: npx prisma migrate dev completes without errors
npm run seed populates all 14 formats
npm run build compiles without TypeScript errors
npm test passes all scoring engine tests
/api/formats returns enriched format list with gameDescription, configOptions, formatCategory
Round creation page shows format description and dynamic config options
Scoring page shows per-player score entry for 2BB/3BB/Lone Ranger/Money Ball formats
Summary page shows team leaderboard for stroke formats and points leaderboard for Chicago
